dental-visit.description: Visit lifecycle, treatment, and chart rules

rules.description: A patient cannot have two active visits simultaneously. Enforced globally per patient (across all branches) via a partial unique index on (patient_id, status) WHERE status='active' plus the findActiveByPatient app guard — stricter than the original 'same branch' wording.
rules.test: dental-visit.test.ts (409 ACTIVE_VISIT_EXISTS on create + on activating a second visit)

rules.description: Visit state transitions are strictly linear: draft → active → completed → locked. No state reversal is permitted.
A visit is immutable after status reaches completed or locked. Chart entries, treatments, prescriptions, and lab orders cannot be added, edited, or deleted.

An empty visit (no treatments, no SOAP content, no attachments) auto-discards instead of completing. Gated behind the default-OFF feature flag dental_visit_auto_discard (V-VIS-004, ADR-010): when OFF (default) empty visits complete normally; when ON, a complete redirects to discarded.
rules.notes: Status corrected not-implemented→implemented-flag-gated (TRACEABILITY 2026-06-08). Default OFF; the discarded state + VISIT_TRANSITIONS active→discarded exist in schema.

rules.description: Treatment state transitions are forward-only: diagnosed → planned → performed → verified. dismissed is reachable from any non-terminal state. No reversal is permitted.
rules.notes: Forward-only enforcement + dismissed-from-any-non-terminal + reversal rejection are tested in treatment.fsm.property.test.ts, dental-visit.treatment-status-transitions.test.ts, and treatment-fsm-http.test.ts (illegal transitions assert 4xx). Status corrected partial→implemented (TRACEABILITY 2026-06-08).

rules.description: A performed/verified treatment is field-immutable — its procedure code, tooth, surface, and price cannot be changed (status transitions remain allowed).
Carry-over creates new dental_treatment rows on the next visit with carriedOver=true, sourceVisitId=<prior visit>, status preserved; not billed until status moves to performed.
rules.test: dental-visit.treatment-templates.test.ts (FR1.11 carries over pending; restores dismissed; 422 on completed visit)

rules.description: Applying a treatment template (POST /visits/{id}/apply-template/{tid}) creates billable treatments and is gated on the clinical role (dentist_owner/dentist_associate), parity with createDentalTreatment; the template must belong to the visit's branch (foreign-branch template → 404, no cross-clinic leak).
rules.notes: Fixed audit 2026-06-08: assertBranchAccess (any member) → assertBranchRole + branch-match guard.

rules.description: Treatment-plan read/accept/version endpoints (GET/POST /patients/{id}/treatment-plan*) authorize against the PATIENT's branch (preferredBranchId via assertPatientBranchAccess), never the caller-supplied branchId query param. A foreign-branch caller passing their own branchId cannot read or snapshot another branch's patient plan.
rules.test: dental-visit.cross-tenant-rbac.test.ts (foreign-branch owner → 403 on plan/accept/version; patient-branch owner → 200)
rules.notes: Fixed audit 2026-06-08 (V-VIS-011): cross-tenant PHI leak — branchId query param was access-checked but never tied to the patient. Mirrors V-PAT-002.

rules.description: An appointment check-in creates a visit record. Deleting the appointment does not delete the visit.
rules.test: dental-scheduling/acceptance.scheduling-workflows.test.ts (AC-SCHED-03 visit preserved on cancel); dental-scheduling.test.ts:548 (visitId linked on check-in)

rules.description: All appointment operations are scoped to a branch (assertBranchRole/assertBranchAccess); a principal with no membership in the target branch is rejected 403.
Walk-in appointments (walkIn=true) bypass the configured working-hours validation.
Cancellation requires a reason (DELETE query param min:5/max:500) -> 422 REASON_REQUIRED when missing/blank.
Non-walk-in appointments must fall within the branch's configured working hours (timezone-aware) -> 422 OUTSIDE_WORKING_HOURS.
Double-booking: soft-warn at create (201 + warnings:[DOUBLE_BOOKING]); hard-block at reschedule (409 RESCHEDULE_CONFLICT). No DB unique constraint (intentional).

An invoice requires at least one performed/verified treatment. Creating an invoice with zero billable treatments → 422 NO_BILLABLE_TREATMENTS. A treatment already billed on a prior invoice → 422 TREATMENT_ALREADY_BILLED (double-billing prevention, S1-T7).
rules.source: createDentalInvoice.ts:46-60 (billable filter status in {performed, verified}; alreadyBilled guard)

rules.description: Tax is always 0. Fee schedule prices are pre-tax. taxRate is server-controlled (NOT caller-supplied — EM-BILL-001 privilege-escalation guard); per-country tax is a Phase-2 stub gated by dental_billing_tax_enabled.
rules.source: createDentalInvoice.ts:69-74 (taxRate/taxCents hardcoded 0, EM-BILL-001 comment — no TODO)
rules.notes: Registry reconciled 2026-06-08: prior source cited a 'TODO comment' that no longer exists; tax is hardcoded with the EM-BILL-001 server-control guard. AC-BIL-004 pins taxCents==0 in all responses.

rules.description: An active payment plan blocks invoice archival (void or uncollectible) → 409 ACTIVE_PAYMENT_PLAN. Staff must resolve the plan before voiding/writing off.

Invoice state lifecycle: draft → issued → paid / partial / overdue / voided. Payments are valid only on issued/partial/overdue (draft → 422 INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION); payment on paid/voided → 422 INVOICE_IMMUTABLE; overpayment → 422 PAYMENT_EXCEEDS_BALANCE.
rules.notes: Registry reconciled 2026-06-08: legacy 'sent' state renamed 'issued' (V-BIL-015); the enum/code/contract all use 'issued'.

rules.description: Dental invoices support an 'uncollectible' terminal status (write-off), reachable from issued/partial/overdue; uncollectible is terminal (no further transitions). Owner-only.
rules.source: dental-billing/markUncollectible.ts; dental-invoice.schema.ts (status enum includes 'uncollectible' + uncollectible_at); route POST /dental/billing/invoices/:invoiceId/uncollectible
rules.notes: Covered by invoice.fsm.property.test.ts (uncollectible reachability + terminality) and dental-billing.test.ts. Registry reconciled 2026-06-05: prior 'deferred' status was stale.

rules.description: A signed consent form is required before invoicing a visit → 422 CONSENT_REQUIRED (V-BIL-007; previously mislabeled BR-011 in code).

Money-integrity bounds (V-BIL-001/002/010): discount percentageRate must be 0–100 (else 422 INVALID_DISCOUNT_RATE — an out-of-range rate drives totals negative); installment count must be 2–24 (else 422 INVALID_INSTALLMENT_COUNT — guards division-by-zero); payment amountCents ≥ 1 (else 422 INVALID_AMOUNT). Each is enforced in the handler as defense-in-depth even if the schema validator is bypassed.

Cross-tenant report scoping: the five billing report/list endpoints whose branchId filter is OPTIONAL (AR aging, collections summary, payer aging, claim worklist, statement batch) MUST scope results to the caller's own active branches when branchId is omitted — NEVER the whole multi-tenant DB. Omitting branchId previously aggregated every org's invoices/payments/claims/balances + patient PHI.
rules.source: getArAging.ts, getCollectionsSummary.ts, getPayerArAging.ts, listInsuranceClaims.ts, generateStatementBatch.ts (getActiveBranchIdsForPerson → inArray scoping); org-billing.facade.ts getActiveBranchIdsForPerson
rules.test: dental-billing.cross-tenant-reports.test.ts (ORG_A caller omitting branchId excludes ORG_B claims/invoices/payments/balances on all 5 endpoints)
rules.notes: Found + fixed 2026-06-08 (dental-billing audit). Same class as V-PAT-002 / V-VIS-011 (caller-supplied branchId not an auth boundary) but the stronger 'omitted → unscoped' variant.

dental-clinical.description: Consent, prescription, and lab order rules

rules.description: Consent form is immutable once signed. No edits, no re-signing (422 CONSENT_FORM_SIGNED), no deletion. The signed record is append-only. signed and revoked are mutually-exclusive terminal states: a signed form cannot be revoked (422 CONSENT_ALREADY_SIGNED) and — symmetrically — a revoked form cannot be signed (422 CONSENT_FORM_REVOKED, V-CLN-010).
Patient registration requires explicit consent (consentGiven: true). Registration is rejected without it.
Prescription creation requires a prescriberMemberId — the ID of a dentist-role member. Non-dentist staff cannot write prescriptions.

Lab order state lifecycle: ordered → in_fabrication → delivered → fitted / cancelled. A terminal order cannot transition to any other state; the `delivered` transition is the LabOrderCompleted (DE-015) marker. (Description corrected from stale ordered→in_progress→completed wording — TRACEABILITY 2026-06-08.)
rules.source: dental-clinical handlers — lab-orders/updateLabOrder.ts; tested in clinical-consent-lab.test.ts

rules.description: A revoked consent form can never become signed (symmetric with the signed→revoke guard). Enforced in three layers: signConsentForm rejects existing.revoked → 422 CONSENT_FORM_REVOKED; ConsentFormRepository.sign() adds revoked=false to its WHERE (race-safe); and the treatment/billing consent gate hasSignedConsentForVisit matches signed=true AND revoked=false so a revoked consent never satisfies the treatment status=performed or billing guard (WF-035: patient revokes → treatment blocked). Closes a consent-integrity hole where revoke-then-sign produced a signed+revoked row that the signed-only gate would have honored.
Branch membership is required for all clinical data access. Every handler calls assertBranchAccess which verifies the requesting user holds a DentalMembership record for the requested branch.

PIN-based local auth lockout: after repeated failed verify-pin attempts the membership is locked (429 with lockedUntil). Lockout escalates from a short delay at 5 attempts to a 5-minute lockout at 10 attempts; a successful verify resets the failed-attempt counter.

Patient registration requires explicit consent (consentGiven: true), else 422 CONSENT_REQUIRED. Single-consent model (V-PAT-004): registration captures ONE consent flag persisted as a JSONB consent object { registrationConsent, capturedAt } on the person record — NOT the legacy 4-flag marketing/data_sharing/sms/email split (never implemented, removed). Per-channel communication consent is a separate endpoint (P1-28).
An archived patient record is read-only. Write operations (update demographics, add follow-up note, create recall) return 403 PATIENT_ARCHIVED.
rules.notes: Enforced on the demographics/follow-up/recall write paths; sub-resource handlers (insurance/contacts/alerts/tasks/household) do not carry the archived-write guard — tracked test/impl backlog.

rules.description: Follow-up notes are append-only — only GET (list) and POST (add) routes exist; there is no PATCH/DELETE route, so edits/deletes cannot occur.
rules.source: dental-patient/engagement/followUpNotes.ts (list + add only); generated routes register GET+POST only
rules.notes: Append-only is enforced structurally (routes never registered) rather than by an explicit 405 guard; no test pins the absence.

rules.description: Clinical records are immutable and append-only. Corrections are additive amendments — the original entry is preserved.
rules.notes: Enforced at API level; no amendment UI yet

rules.description: Patient merge and unmerge are deferred (Phase 2). Both POST /patients/merge and POST /patients/unmerge are admin-only and return a clean 501 NOT_IMPLEMENTED by design; duplicates are managed manually (detectDuplicatePatients surfaces candidates, registration returns a non-blocking 201 + warning rather than 409). The legacy dental_patient_merge_enabled feature flag is not present in code.

Completing one treatment-plan item must NOT complete the whole plan unless ALL items are complete. Plan status is derived from linked treatments: all done→completed, some done→partially_completed, none→approved; dismissed/declined treatments are excluded from the denominator.

dental-pmd.description: Portable Medical Document (canonical expansion; V-PMD-009) rules — per-visit immutable signed snapshots, external PMD import + immutability, checksum integrity, identity binding, patient-self access, continuity-of-care export.

rules.description: A PMD is an immutable per-visit snapshot generated ONLY from a completed or locked visit (otherwise 422 VISIT_NOT_COMPLETED). Content is SHA-256 checksummed (sha256-<hex>) and sealed; future changes to the source visit do NOT alter the PMD. Re-generation supersedes the prior row (generated→superseded) and never mutates a superseded row's content/checksum.

An imported external PMD is stored verbatim and is READ-ONLY after creation. PATCH/PUT/DELETE on /dental/pmd/imported/:id are rejected at the route level with 405 IMPORTED_PMD_IMMUTABLE (not 403 — there is no GET-only generated mutate route; the guard lives in app.ts). Its data is never auto-merged into the patient's editable records; the Safety Floor merge is add-only and never mutates imported content.

Identity binding: the PMD's patientId is derived from the VISIT (single source of truth), never the request body. A body.patientId disagreeing with visit.patientId → 422 PATIENT_VISIT_MISMATCH; the checksum-sealed content always carries visit.patientId so no client-controllable id can leak into a non-repudiation record.

Import checksum verification: when body.checksum is supplied, the server recomputes sha256-<hex> of body.content and rejects a mismatch with 422 CHECKSUM_MISMATCH. checksum is optional (import without one → 201).

sourceDescription is REQUIRED on import (identifies the originating software system, e.g. 'Open Dental v21.1') for data-provenance audit; persisted and returned. Capped at 200 chars (V-PMD-010) → 422 VALIDATION_ERROR if exceeded.

PHI data-movement logging: import (pmd.import), generation (pmd.generate + pmd.generated DE-017), and export (pmd.export) each write a synchronous dental_audit_log row (ADR-006; no event bus). A failed action writes NO row.
tests: dental-pmd-events.test.ts (pmd.generated / pmd.import / pmd.export rows + negative paths)

rules.description: Patient-self access (HIPAA right-of-access, EF-PMD-007): a user whose id === patient.person may read/list/export/download their OWN PMDs and care-record without a branch membership. All other readers require branch access derived from the resource (visit.branchId / patient.preferredBranchId); a full-role member of a DIFFERENT branch is denied (cross-branch PHI isolation).
rules.source: getPMDForVisit / listPMDs / listImportedPMDs / exportPMD / exportPatientCareRecord (isPatientSelf branch + assertBranchAccess)

dental-perio.description: Periodontal charting: one-chart-per-visit, FDI/depth validation, role gate, visit-lock cascade, AAP/EFP 2017 staging/grading

rules.description: One perio chart per visit. A second create for the same visitId returns 409 CHART_EXISTS.

Perio chart is immutable once the parent visit is completed/locked. Writes (create/upsert/complete) on a sealed visit are rejected: create→422 VISIT_LOCKED, upsert→422 VISIT_IMMUTABLE, complete→422 VISIT_LOCKED. A completed/locked chart itself rejects further writes as a 409 CHART_COMPLETED state conflict (N-PER-01).
Probing depths must be integers 0-20mm per site; recession -5..20mm. Out of range → 422 INVALID_DEPTH.

FDI tooth number must fall in a valid quadrant range (adult 11-18/21-28/31-38/41-48; primary 51-55/61-65/71-75/81-85). Cross-quadrant gaps (e.g. 19) → 422 INVALID_TOOTH_NUMBER. The TypeSpec @minValue/@maxValue only bounds 11-85; the precise disjoint check is in the handler.

Chart create/upsert/complete require a clinical role (dentist_owner, dentist_associate, hygienist) on the chart's branch. Non-clinical branch members (staff_scheduling/staff_full) and non-members → 403. Read (get/list) additionally allows staff_full; staff_scheduling is excluded from all perio access (EF-PER-002).

Tooth-reading upsert is idempotent on (chartId, toothNumber). A single-site PATCH writes only the columns present in the request (one atomic onConflictDoUpdate); other sites on the tooth are never replaced to null. No 409 on duplicate.
Completing a chart requires a minimum reading count: adult 16, primary (FDI 51-85) 8 (dentition inferred from charted tooth numbers). Below the minimum → 422 INSUFFICIENT_READINGS.

Visit-lock → chart-lock cascade. The chart `locked` state is materialized lazily on the next perio read/write: when the parent visit is completed/locked, a not-yet-locked chart transitions to `locked` and a perio.chart.locked audit marker is written (ADR-006: audit-log-only, no event bus).

On completion the chart is classified per the 2017 AAP/EFP system (stage from worst interdental CAL + complexity factors; grade from %bone-loss/age + smoking/diabetes modifiers; extent). Stage/extent are null without evidence; grade defaults to B. IDEAL-§343: remainingTeeth is NOT inferred from the charted-tooth count — a partial chart of a fully-dentate patient is not over-staged to IV via the <20-teeth factor.
rules.source: utils/perio-staging.ts; utils/perio-classify-chart.ts:118; completePerioChart.ts:126; perio-staging.test.ts; perio-classify-chart.test.ts (IDEAL-§343 case)

dental-imaging.description: Imaging workspace, finding state machine, and ceph analysis rules

rules.description: Annotations are non-destructive overlays on top of immutable images. Geometry is stored in imaging_annotation.data (JSONB); the underlying image record is never mutated.
Panoramic images display a measurement-accuracy warning until the branch has set calibration data for that image.
rules.notes: Warning delivery is Phase 3a; calibration endpoint exists

rules.description: An image must be linked to a patient (patient_id NOT NULL). It may optionally be linked to a visit and/or specific teeth via join table.
Image deletion is default-deny. Only dentist and associate roles may delete images. All other roles receive 403.
An associate may only delete images they personally acquired (acquiredBy === user.id). Attempting to delete another user's image returns 403.
Image deletion is soft-only. DELETE sets status='archived'; no row is physically removed.
Branch isolation applies to all 13 imaging endpoints. Every handler calls assertBranchAccess. Finding details are hidden from users without branch membership.
Legacy dental_attachment records are surfaced as imaging items via a union adapter in listPatientImages. Document and deleted attachments are excluded.
Images are cached locally in IndexedDB for offline access in the imaging workspace.
Image modality is non-nullable with default 'other'. Applies to both study and individual image records.
Maximum upload file size is 100 MB. Enforcement is delegated to the storage multipart layer.
rules.notes: Handler delegates; no explicit byte-count guard at handler level

rules.description: Allowed image formats: JPEG, PNG, TIFF, BMP. Uploads with other MIME types are rejected with 422.
Concurrent annotation writes use last-write-wins (no optimistic locking). The most recently committed write takes effect.

Cephalometric (and CBCT) features require the 'addon' imaging tier. The gate is strict-equality `imagingTier !== 'addon'` (BR-016c), so BOTH 'free' AND 'basic' are blocked with 403 IMAGING_TIER_REQUIRED — 'basic' is 2-D-only and does NOT unlock ceph. Enforced before any DB write; cephalometric/CBCT study CREATE is gated too, not just downstream ceph endpoints.
rules.source: All CephMgmt_*.ts handlers + createImagingStudy.ts — getImagingTierForBranch, `!== 'addon'` check
rules.notes: Corrects the prior 'free or null' wording which omitted the 'basic'-blocked case (proved by imaging-coverage.test.ts EF-IMG-009 basic→403).

rules.description: A null/absent imagingTier is treated as no-access (blocked, 403) — never interpreted as unlimited access. Identical outcome to 'free' and 'basic': only an explicit 'addon' unlocks ceph/CBCT.
rules.source: All CephMgmt_*.ts handlers — `!== 'addon'` is true for null

rules.description: Ceph landmark status transitions are strictly forward: placed → confirmed → locked. locked is terminal — no further status changes are permitted.
A locked landmark is fully immutable. Any PATCH to coordinates or status, or DELETE, returns 422 with code LANDMARK_LOCKED.
Invalid landmark status transitions (e.g., confirmed → placed, locked → confirmed) are rejected with 422 and code INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION.
Ceph report generation is gated on four required landmarks (A, B, Go, Po) all having status confirmed. Attempting to generate a report without all four confirmed returns a validation error.
Branch isolation applies to all ceph endpoints. A non-member requesting any CephMgmt_* endpoint receives 404 (not 403) to avoid leaking branch existence.
Ceph reports are immutable versioned snapshots. Each report has a monotonically incrementing version per image (1, 2, 3…). No update or delete handlers exist.
Every write handler (batchUpsertCephLandmarks, updateCephLandmark) triggers an inline analysis recompute and returns { items, analysis }. Analysis is always fresh after any landmark write.

analysisType is selected by the ?analysisType= query param and defaults to 'steiner_hybrid_sn'. Six protocols ship (steiner_hybrid_sn, ricketts, downs, tweed, mcnamara, jarabak); an unknown value is rejected 422 UNSUPPORTED_ANALYSIS_TYPE (never silently defaulted — showing the wrong analysis is a safety bug). The response echoes the requested analysisType.
rules.notes: Supersedes the prior v1.4 'only steiner_hybrid_sn' claim — multi-analysis shipped.

rules.description: createCephReport returns 201 with { id, imageId, version, snapshot, createdAt }. The snapshot is a frozen JSON blob containing all measurement data at creation time and never changes afterwards.
Version numbers for ceph reports are monotonically increasing per image (1, 2, 3…). A DB unique constraint on (imageId, version) prevents duplicate versions.
rules.source: imaging_ceph.schema.ts — unique index on (imageId, version); createReportVersion()

rules.description: updateCephLandmark (PATCH) returns { items, analysis } — the full landmark list plus recomputed analysis, not just the patched landmark.
A locked landmark is immutable across all three mutation axes: coordinate PATCH → 422 LANDMARK_LOCKED; status PATCH → 422 LANDMARK_LOCKED; DELETE → 422 LANDMARK_LOCKED.
recomputeCephAnalysis (POST /analysis/recompute) returns a single CephAnalysis object — not { items, analysis }. The endpoint is idempotent.

Ceph landmark batch upsert is idempotent — upserting the same landmark id with new coordinates replaces the existing entry without creating a duplicate row. Upsert semantics are enforced at the DB layer via onConflictDoUpdate.

A batch upsert of landmarks is rejected in its entirety if any landmark in the batch is locked. The handler skips locked landmarks silently (no partial application, no error). This protects locked entries without requiring the caller to pre-filter.

recomputeCephAnalysis is idempotent — calling it multiple times with unchanged landmark state produces identical measurement output each time. It writes to imagingCephAnalyses only and never creates a report row.

Calibration provenance is captured in the report snapshot at creation time. The pixelSpacingMm value used to compute measurements is frozen into snapshot.calibration. Subsequent calibration changes do not retroactively update existing report snapshots.
rules.source: CephMgmt_createCephReport.ts snapshot builder — calibration: { value, method }

rules.description: Ceph analysis requires calibration (pixelSpacingMm > 0) to display mm-unit measurements. When pixelSpacingMm is null or 0, raw pixel distances are computed but the calibration method is 'not_calibrated' — clients must label uncalibrated measurements visually to prevent clinical misinterpretation.
Any CephMgmt_* operation on a non-existent image returns 404. Non-members also receive 404 (not 403) to avoid leaking image existence. 403 is never returned for missing images.
rules.source: All CephMgmt_*.ts handlers — assertBranchAccess produces 404; ImagingRepository.getById returns 404

rules.description: Ceph report version numbers are monotonically increasing per image (1, 2, 3…). Gaps in version numbers are not permitted. A DB unique constraint on (imageId, version) enforces this and makes concurrent report creation race-safe.

The steiner_hybrid_sn label is the mandatory analysis type for all v1.4 ceph analysis responses. Deviating from this label in analysis computations or response payloads is a schema violation. No other analysis type exists in v1.4.
rules.source: All CephMgmt_*.ts handlers returning analysis; @monobase/ceph-math computeCephAnalysis

rules.description: DELETE /dental/imaging/images/:imageId/ceph/landmarks/:landmarkId is permitted on landmarks with status confirmed or placed. Only locked landmarks are fully protected from deletion (returns 422 LANDMARK_LOCKED). Successful deletion resets the analysis state (triggers recompute or marks stale).
rules.source: CephMgmt_deleteCephLandmark.ts — lock check; CIMG-014 lock matrix

rules.description: Ceph report snapshot measurement fields (sna, snb, anb, witsAppraisal, etc.) are nullable. If a required landmark is absent at report-creation time, the corresponding measurement is null in the snapshot rather than blocking report creation. Missing measurements produce null values — not errors.
rules.source: CephMgmt_createCephReport.ts snapshot — measurements from computeCephAnalysis; D-4 snapshot completeness

rules.description: After a landmark's coordinates are updated via PATCH, the ceph analysis is marked stale. The analysis is not automatically recomputed — the user must explicitly trigger POST /analysis/recompute. Until recomputed, the { items, analysis } response from updateCephLandmark reflects the freshly recomputed state inline (CIMG-009), but subsequent getCephAnalysis reads may reflect pre-patch data until a standalone recompute is triggered.
rules.source: CephMgmt_updateCephLandmark.ts — inline recompute on write (CIMG-009); CephMgmt_recomputeCephAnalysis.ts

rules.description: All image-scoped CephMgmt_* routes require a valid imageId that exists in the imagingImages table. A request for a ceph operation on an imageId that does not exist returns 404. There is no study-level ceph endpoint — all ceph operations are image-scoped.
